title: deschampsia cespitosa subsp. levis
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. levis, also known as mountain hair grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe and protect the skin from minor wounds and irritations. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties to prevent infection. Mountain hair grass can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
